name: append_other_augment_extends_range
code:
proof fn phi_1_append_other_augment_extends_range<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    new_updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    new_rid: ReqId,
    log_entry: LogEntry<DT>,
    logUpper: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logUpper),
        log_entry.node_id != nodeId,
        new_updates.contains_key(new_rid),
        new_updates[new_rid] === (UpdateState::Placed { op: log_entry.op, idx: logUpper }),
        !queue.contains(new_rid),
        forall |rid| #[trigger] updates.contains_key(rid) && rid != new_rid ==> new_updates.contains_key(rid) && new_updates[rid] === updates[rid],
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, 0, logUpper, nodeId, updates),
    ensures ({
        let new_log = log.insert(logUpper, log_entry);
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, new_log, 0, 0, logUpper + 1, nodeId, new_updates)
    }),
{
    LogRangeMatchesQueue_append_other_augment(queue, log, log.insert(logUpper, log_entry), 0, 0, logUpper, nodeId, updates, new_updates, new_rid, log_entry);
}
reason: Appending a remote entry extends the log range by 1 without changing the queue — tests the augment variant that both preserves existing entries and extends the range

name: remote_entry_no_queue_consumption
code:
proof fn phi_2_remote_entry_no_queue_consumption<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    remoteId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    logIdx: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logIdx + 1),
        log[logIdx].node_id == remoteId,
        remoteId != nodeId,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logIdx, logIdx + 1, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        queue.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A single remote entry skips the queue entirely — the recursion steps to `[logIdx+1, logIdx+1)` which forces `queueIndex == queue.len()`

name: local_entry_placed_idx_link
code:
proof fn phi_3_local_entry_placed_idx_link<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    logIdx: LogIdx,
)
    requires
        LogContainsEntriesUpToHere(log, logIdx + 1),
        log[logIdx].node_id == nodeId,
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, 0, logIdx, logIdx + 1, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        queue.len() == 1,
        updates.contains_key(queue[0]),
        updates[queue[0]] is Placed,
        updates[queue[0]].arrow_Placed_idx() == logIdx,
{
}
reason: A single local entry links exactly one queue element's Placed index to the log position — tests the core matching invariant

name: empty_range_exhausts_queue
code:
proof fn phi_4_empty_range_exhausts_queue<DT: Dispatch>(
    queue: Seq<ReqId>,
    log: Map<LogIdx, LogEntry<DT>>,
    nodeId: NodeId,
    updates: Map<ReqId, UpdateState<DT>>,
    idx: LogIdx,
    qi: nat,
)
    requires
        qi <= queue.len(),
        LogRangeMatchesQueue(queue, log, qi, idx, idx, nodeId, updates),
    ensures
        qi == queue.len(),
{
}
reason: Empty log range forces queue index to equal queue length — base case of the recursion

name: dispatch_trait_trusted_no_constraints
reason: `Dispatch` is `#[verus::trusted]` — associated types are unconstrained, meaning any implementation could introduce unsound type relationships
